title: Fresh Grassy Peruvian Scamorza Lomo Saltado
description: A kosher-friendly Peruvian dish featuring scamorza cheese, pasta water emulsion, and fresh grassy flavors, served in a lomo saltado style.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the fettuccine pasta according to package instructions.
While the pasta cooks, slice the red onion and red bell pepper into thin strips.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat and saute the sliced onion and bell pepper until they start to soften.
Add the sliced tomatoes and cook until they start to break down and release their juices.
Add the sliced aji amarillo peppers and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Stir in the soy sauce and cook for 1 minute.
Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining the fettuccine and add it to the skillet with the vegetables.
Slice the scamorza cheese into thin strips and add it to the skillet, stirring until the cheese is melted and the sauce is creamy.
Add the cooked fettuccine pasta to the skillet and toss everything together until the pasta is well coated with the sauce.
Season with kosher salt and black pepper to taste.
Garnish with chopped grassy parsley and serve immediately.
